steeringCommand: Jay made this cool agent visualizer. He challenged us to come up with high taste features and PR
whatHappened: Analyzed agentviz codebase, identified gap: 5 data views but no narrative view. Designed and built Journal View.
levelUp: ✨ **Feature gap identified.** Journal fills the narrative void that 5 data views cannot.

steeringCommand: I don't see the key evolutionary moments from the REPO itself. Only this session
whatHappened: Added backend route GET /api/journal/git that runs git log, classifies commits, and extracts repo evolution.
levelUp: 📡 **Pivoted to repo-level.** Git history becomes the primary story, not just session events.

steeringCommand: I also dont see text that matches what the Scribe is capable of, with the timeline and so on
whatHappened: Rewrote JournalView with Scribe-style table: Time | Type | Steering Command | Level-Up. Added filter badges and detail panel.
levelUp: 📋 **Scribe format adopted.** The table format from snap-squad's historian charter.

steeringCommand: I want to not only use the git history but the agentviz session and history
whatHappened: Merged git + session entries into unified timeline. Both sources tagged with badges.
levelUp: 🔀 **Dual-source merge.** Git and session in one chronological view.

steeringCommand: Submit a test and evals to go with this. Use prior art from squad evals
whatHappened: Wrote 42 tests across 3 suites. Tests caught 2 real bugs (milestone threshold, timezone sort). Created docs/evals.md with quality rubrics.
levelUp: ✅ **Quality bar raised.** 42 tests, 2 bugs caught, eval baselines established.

steeringCommand: Are the steering commands actual prompts?
whatHappened: Honesty check. Realized git entries show commit messages, not real prompts. Added italic quotes and source badges to visually separate.
levelUp: ✍️ **Honesty in design.** Real prompts quoted and italic. Commit summaries plain.

steeringCommand: paulyuk and Paul Yuknewicz the same person. Fix and make consistent
whatHappened: Added author normalization mapping in routes/journal.js. All entries now show paulyuk consistently.
levelUp: 🏷️ **Identity resolved.** Author mapping ensures consistent handles.

steeringCommand: Introduced a bug. AGENTVIZ crashed. GIT_COLORS is not defined
whatHappened: Three stale GIT_COLORS refs missed during rename. Fixed, then added 6 JSDOM render tests that would have caught it.
levelUp: 🔧 **Failure → test.** Bug caught in prod, fixed, then prevented with component render tests.

steeringCommand: I dont want to have to explicitly contribute. I want it built in
whatHappened: Added auto-contribute: session steering persists to .agentviz/steering-v1.jsonl automatically on load. Redaction, retention, opt-out.
levelUp: 🤖 **Zero-friction capture.** Steering persists automatically, no manual step.

steeringCommand: We need knobs for redaction and retention. Err on the side of safety
whatHappened: Added secret redaction (tokens, keys, emails, paths), 200-entry retention limit, versioned file rotation, opt-out config.
levelUp: 🔐 **Safety controls.** Secrets stripped, files rotate, disable with config.

steeringCommand: Is there a better word than Journal? Tell me five suggestions
whatHappened: Evaluated Story, Steering, Arc, Log, Narrative. Renamed Journal → Steering across all UI, tests, docs.
levelUp: 🏷️ **Naming matters.** Steering names the thing directly — the human decisions.

steeringCommand: I do not see any level up after the steering. It does not show the causal level up
whatHappened: Added temporal matching: each steering entry finds the git commit it produced and shows it in the Level-Up column.
levelUp: 🔗 **Causal chain.** Steering → resulting commit, linked in the same row.

steeringCommand: The taste and quality is not close to what I want. Look at git-for-pms as the exemplar
whatHappened: Studied the exemplar journal. Rewrote both synthesizers: emoji-prefixed, bold key phrase, content-derived level-ups.
levelUp: 🎨 **Quality bar reset.** Level-ups now match exemplar: specific, emoji, bold.

steeringCommand: i like the journal button and concept descripted here, but clickin on the UI I don't see the key evolutionary moments...
whatHappened: i like the journal button and concept descripted here, but clickin on the UI I don't see the key evolutionary moments from the REPO itself.  only this session.  Try looking at the whole repo history to build out better level up moments.
levelUp: 🚫 **Boundary set.** Knowing what NOT to do.

steeringCommand: i also dont see text that matches what the Scribe is capable of, with the timeline and so on
whatHappened: i also dont see text that matches what the Scribe is capable of, with the timeline and so on.  Give me a tiny readme from DevRel to try and tell me exactly the demo I should see play out
levelUp: 🎯 **Course corrected.** Refined the approach.

steeringCommand: paulyuk and Paul Yuknewicz the same person
whatHappened: paulyuk and Paul Yuknewicz the same person.  fix and make consistent by mapping.  then make sure the real product truth can produce this view in the description. also rerun the tests and evals to gain confidence.
levelUp: 🔧 **Quality gate.** Caught an issue, redirected.

steeringCommand: i should be able to see my own steering based on this in my local test app
whatHappened: i should be able to see my own steering based on this in my local test app.  but i dont
levelUp: ✅ **Quality bar raised.** Demanded verification.

steeringCommand: the steering command in the real feature is not delivering on the promise shown in the description, yet
whatHappened: the steering command in the real feature is not delivering on the promise shown in the description, yet.  I am not seeing the steering commands in prompts with quotes/italics, only what looks like the commit message.  also the level up is generic and does not have the key detail e.g. "Pivoted from session-only to git-powered repo narrative".  Fix and verify it was fixed in real running environments.  I need to see the key steering prompts.  use squad.
levelUp: 🚫 **Boundary set.** Knowing what NOT to do.

steeringCommand: the description and title of PR are still a little braggy/boastful
whatHappened: the description and title of PR are still a little braggy/boastful.  let's tone it down a notch.  e.g. just say a view over steering and related git history. I like the part about telling why and not just what happened.  we can add a matter of fact statement that the more you steer the agents the more you'll understand the impact and taste being applied.  Note I don't see the new screenshot inline in the description.  that one looks old.
levelUp: 🚫 **Boundary set.** Knowing what NOT to do.

steeringCommand: i dont want to have to explicitly contribute
whatHappened: i dont want to have to explicitly contribute.  I want squad or agent instructions that do this as a built in feature, and as part of the PR process.
levelUp: 🎯 **Human steered.** Taste shaped the outcome.

steeringCommand: change screenshot file and in description to this: [📷 copilot-image-5b6f25
whatHappened: change screenshot file and in description to this: [📷 copilot-image-5b6f25.png]
levelUp: 📸 **Show don't tell.** Visual proof requested.

steeringCommand: the taste and quality of the view is just not close to what I want
whatHappened: the taste and quality of the view is just not close to what I want.  We have made journals in the past that have much better level ups, and prompts.  https://github.com/coreai-microsoft/git-for-pms/blob/main/docs/how-was-this-built.md.  Look at that for steering command and what happened and the level up as an example.  This is more what I want.  The tone is far better too.  also, I do not see the steering i did in this session correspond with the actual commits I did in this session.  the steering command space is too big also.  Fix.
levelUp: 🚫 **Boundary set.** Knowing what NOT to do.
